mission.title: kube-ovn: [BUG] Unable to start on Talos Linux
mission.description: [BUG] Unable to start on Talos Linux. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kube-ovn troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kube-ovn deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-ovn
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-ovn -n kube-system --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kube-ovn configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kube-ovn configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-ovn
kubectl get configmap -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kube-ovn
```
When the `ovs-ovn` daemonset tries to start the `openvswitch` container fails to start with this error:

```

```

I have tried changing the securityContext to run this container privileged and as root but it still has this error.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [BUG] Unable to start on Talos Linux
steps.description:
I used Cozystack's image and setup Talos to use Kube-OVN, I still get cryptics errors concerning iptables:

```
failed to create iptables rule "-m set --match-set ovn40subnets src -j ACCEPT": running [/usr/local/sbin/iptables -t filter -I INPUT 1 -m set --match-set ovn40subnets src -j ACCEPT
```yaml
/kube-ovn/start-ovs.sh: line 83: /usr/local/sbin/iptables: Operation not permitted
```

steps.title: Upgrade kube-ovn to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kube-ovn:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kube-ovn kube-ovn/kube-ovn --namespace kube-system
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system
helm list -n kube-system
```

steps.title: Confirm [BUG] Unable to start on Talos Linux is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-ovn -n kube-system --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kube-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
I used Cozystack's image and setup Talos to use Kube-OVN, I still get cryptics errors concerning iptables:

```
failed to create iptables rule "-m set --match-set ovn40subnets src -j ACCEPT": running [/usr/local/sbin/iptables -t filter -I INPUT 1 -m set --match-set ovn40subnets src -j ACCEPT --wait]: exit status 1: iptables v1.8.10 (nf_tables): Can't open socket to ipset.
codeSnippets:
---
cni_conf:
  CNI_CONFIG_PRIORITY: "10"
  MOUNT_LOCAL_BIN_DIR: false
func:
  ENABLE_NP: false
ipv4:
  POD_CIDR: "10.131.0.0/16"
  POD_GATEWAY: "10.131.0.1"
  SVC_CIDR: "10.128.0.0/20"
  JOIN_CIDR: "10.130.0.0/24"
  # PINGER_EXTERNAL_ADDRESS: "1.1.1.1"
  # PINGER_EXTERNAL_DOMAIN: "kube-ovn.io."
namespace: "kube-system"
DISABLE_MODULES_MANAGEMENT: true
OPENVSWITCH_DIR: "/var/kube-ovn/openvswitch"
OVN_DIR: "/var/kube-ovn/ovn"

securityContext:
     runAsUser: 0
     privileged: true
     capabilities:
       add:
         - NET_ADMIN
         - NET_BIND_SERVICE
         - SYS_MODULE
         - SYS_NICE
         - SYS_ADMIN

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with Kube-OVN installed as the CNI plugin.
